2012-05-01.title: Israeli Defense Minister Keeps All Options Open on Iran
2012-05-01.abstract: Ehud Barak offered a dim view of talks on Iran’s nuclear program and said all options, including a military strike, remained on the table despite weeks of criticism over the stance.

2012-05-01.title: Role of Torture Revisited in Bin Laden Narrative
2012-05-01.abstract: The Democratic senators who lead two Senate committees rebutted a former C.I.A. official’s defense of the Bush administration interrogation program.

2012-05-01.title: Top U.S. Security Official Says ‘Rigorous Standards’ Are Used for Drone Strikes
2012-05-01.abstract: John O. Brennan, President Obama’s top counterterrorism adviser, said strikes targeting suspected militants always come after extensive deliberations.

2012-05-01.title: Details Are Refuted in Tale of Bo Guagua’s Red Ferrari
2012-05-01.abstract: Accounts of Bo Guagua’s reportedly lavish arrival at Jon M. Huntsman Jr.’s home in China last spring have been disputed by several people involved.

2012-05-01.title: Tough Questions for Cameron on Ties to Murdoch
2012-05-01.abstract: The widening scandal involving Murdoch media interests is seen as having potentially serious political repercussions for the Cameron government.

2012-05-02.title: Text of Obama’s Speech in Afghanistan
2012-05-02.abstract: A text of President Obama’s speech during his unannounced trip to Kabul, Afghanistan, as released by the White House.

2012-05-02.title: Group Seeks Suspension of Iran From I.M.F.
2012-05-02.abstract: An American advocacy group is seeking to pressure the International Monetary Fund to withdraw all its holdings in Iran’s central bank or to suspend Iranian membership.

2012-05-02.title: Austerity Pain Fills Europe With Protests on May Day
2012-05-02.abstract: Urging alternatives for economic growth, tens of thousands of demonstrators rallied in Spain, Britain, France, Greece, Germany and beyond.

2012-05-02.title: European Financial Treaty Faces Skeptical Irish Voters in Tough Referendum Fight
2012-05-02.abstract: Advocates say a “no” vote on the treaty could hurt Ireland’s ability to borrow internationally, while opponents say the pact’s limits on deficits and debt could prolong Ireland’s slump.

2012-05-02.title: Baseball in Their Veins, but a New Ball at Their Feet
2012-05-02.abstract: Even with a less storied past than baseball, which has long been considered as Cuban as hand-rolled cigars, soccer has made inroads in Cuba in recent years.

2012-05-03.title: Israel Military Clears Soldiers in Deaths of Gaza Civilians
2012-05-03.abstract: The Israeli military said on Wednesday that it had closed its inquiry into the deaths of at least 21 Palestinian family members during the 2009 Gaza war, and denied charges that forces deliberately attacked civilians.

2012-05-03.title: Britain’s Biggest Satellite Broadcaster Distances Itself From Murdoch
2012-05-03.abstract: Following a scathing British parliamentary report declaring Rupert Murdoch unfit to run a media empire, BSkyB insisted on Wednesday that its own track record should determine fitness to hold a broadcasting license.

2012-05-03.title: France Approaches the End of a Presidential Campaign With a Bitter Debate
2012-05-03.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy of France and his Socialist challenger, François Hollande, clashed on Wednesday night in their only televised debate before Sunday’s election.

2012-05-03.title: A Car Chase, Secret Talks and Second Thoughts
2012-05-03.abstract: Details of intrigue, heroics and ultimately what some people involved called a betrayal emerged from Chen Guangcheng’s saga.

2012-05-03.title: Fallout of Bin Laden Raid: Aid Groups in Pakistan Are Suspect
2012-05-03.abstract: Humanitarian work that helped millions of Pakistanis was compromised after it emerged that Dr. Shakil Afridi ran a vaccination scheme to help the C.I.A. locate Osama bin Laden.

2012-05-04.title: Brazil Sending More Troops to Guard Amazon Borders
2012-05-04.abstract: In an effort to protect its wealth of resources, Brazil is increasing its military presence along the Amazon borders near Venezuela, Suriname, French Guiana and Guyana.

2012-05-04.title: Results of French and Greek Elections Could Signal Shift on Economic Crisis
2012-05-04.abstract: The outcome of Sunday’s elections is likely to be another blow to a German-designed austerity plan to cure the euro zone’s debt and banking crisis.

2012-05-04.title: Dissident’s Plea for Protection From China Deepens Crisis
2012-05-04.abstract: Facing criticism amid fraying relations with China, American officials privately acknowledged missteps in the handling of the Chinese dissident Chen Guangcheng.

2012-05-04.title: A Show of Solidarity for Hunger Strikers
2012-05-04.abstract: Scores of Palestinian prisoners have joined a hunger strike that officials say now counts more than 1,500 participants.

2012-05-04.title: Despite Chaos Above Ground, Cairo’s Metro System Runs Smoothly
2012-05-04.abstract: The Cairo subway continues to offer residents consistent, efficient service, while most others suffer in the year since the revolution.

2012-05-05.title: Serbia, at a Crossroads, Prepares for Elections
2012-05-05.abstract: Facing a challenge from the right, the pro-Western president, Boris Tadic, will ask Serbian voters for a fresh mandate in voting on Sunday.

2012-05-05.title: Head of Drill Sergeant School Reinstated
2012-05-05.abstract: A command sergeant major is vindicated after being suspended under a cloud

2012-05-05.title: In Elections, Greeks Are Expected to Make Old Guard Pay for Turmoil
2012-05-05.abstract: Along with French elections, and with a rising tide of anti-austerity sentiment across Europe, Greece’s vote is expected to have a clear impact on the future of the euro.

2012-05-05.title: For China, a Dissident in Exile Is One Less Headache Back Home
2012-05-05.abstract: Once exiled, nettlesome prisoners of conscience, like Chen Guangcheng, almost invariably lose their ability to grab headlines in the West and to command widespread sympathy both in China and abroad.

2012-05-05.title: Aleppo Clash Poses Risk of Widening Syrian Revolt
2012-05-05.abstract: A violent crackdown on protesters in the country’s largest city could complicate matters for President Bashar al-Assad, who has deftly maneuvered to stay in power throughout the uprising.

2012-05-06.title: A Killing Reverberates From a Cambodian Forest
2012-05-06.abstract: In another time, in a less connected era, the killing of a lone environmentalist in a remote forest somewhere in Asia would have gone largely unnoticed: another voice gone quiet. Not this time. Not with the death of Chut Wutty in Cambodia.

2012-05-06.title: Round-Trip Tickets for Chen Could Upgrade U.S.-China Relations
2012-05-06.abstract: The China scholar Orville Schell suggests that Beijing might do well to offer assurances to the human rights lawyer Chen Guangcheng that he and his family will be able to return home after he goes abroad for legal studies. Such a gesture would be welcomed by Washington and a possible boon to U.S.-China relations.

2012-05-06.title: Denouement in France
2012-05-06.abstract: French voters began going to the polls Sunday morning in what has been characterized as one of the most dramatic and consequential elections in the postwar era, with the Socialist candidate, François Hollande, widely expected to defeat the incumbent, President Nicolas Sarkozy.

2012-05-06.title: Hollande Ousts Sarkozy in French Presidential Election
2012-05-06.abstract: François Hollande became the first Socialist president of France since 1981, but his victory over Nicolas Sarkozy will also be seen as a challenge to the policy of austerity in the euro zone.

2012-05-06.title: Greek Voters Punish 2 Main Parties for Collapse
2012-05-06.abstract: In parliamentary elections, voters sent a message to traditional governing parties, which are being blamed for the country’s economic woes, and bolstered parties on the left and right.

2012-05-07.title: Rebels Hold French Journalist in Colombia
2012-05-07.abstract: Romeo Langlois, who was working for a French television channel, disappeared on April 28 after a gunfight involving FARC guerrillas, who claimed Sunday to have him in custody.

2012-05-07.title: In Rise and Fall of China’s Bo Xilai, an Arc of Ruthlessness
2012-05-07.abstract: Bo Xilai’s talents were counterbalanced by what friends and critics alike say was an insatiable ambition and studied indifference to the wrecked lives that littered his path to power.

2012-05-07.title: France’s President-Elect Has Fans Aplenty at Home
2012-05-07.abstract: François Hollande, who was the mayor of Tulle, France, for seven years, has won praise there for his common touch.

2012-05-07.title: Socialist Wins in France
2012-05-07.abstract: François Hollande defeated President Nicolas Sarkozy on Sunday, becoming the first Socialist elected president of France since François Mitterrand.

2012-05-07.title: Arab Spring Spurs Palestinian Journalists to Test Free Speech Limits
2012-05-07.abstract: Palestinian journalists and activists, imbued with the spirit of the Arab Spring, have become more daring, but the instinct of some in the Palestinian Authority has been to crack down.

2012-05-08.title: French Center-Right Assesses Two Unpalatable Paths
2012-05-08.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy’s party must consider allying itself with the far right or holding to the center and hoping against further losses in June.

2012-05-08.title: Pinched Aspirations of Iran’s Young Multitudes
2012-05-08.abstract: Young adults in Iran who came of age after the revolution have resigned themselves to making the best of a bad situation.

2012-05-08.title: China Expels Al Jazeera Channel
2012-05-08.abstract: The expulsion, reportedly over official anger about a documentary on forced labor, among other programs, was seen as a signal of growing sensitivity about foreign news coverage.

2012-05-08.title: Israeli Court Rejects the Release of Hunger Strikers
2012-05-08.abstract: The Supreme Court, citing secret evidence of militant ties, refused to release two Palestinians held without charge and who have been on hunger strike for 69 days.

2012-05-08.title: Dead Dolphins and Birds Are Causing Alarm in Peru
2012-05-08.abstract: Peruvian officials have not shared the results of tests to determine why dolphins and seabirds are dying, causing some to fear there is more to the story.

2012-05-09.title: Indians Host Clinton While Also Wooing Iran
2012-05-09.abstract: This week’s diplomatic choreography — with Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton and the Iranians in New Delhi — provided a window into the realpolitik of sanctions against Iran.

2012-05-09.title: An Effort to Bury a Throwaway Culture One Repair at a Time
2012-05-09.abstract: Amsterdam’s Repair Cafe encourages people to bring old items that they might have otherwise thrown out to have them restored by expert volunteers.

2012-05-09.title: Avoiding a ‘Prescribed Version’ of China
2012-05-09.abstract: When the photographer Rian Dundon went to China, he was intent on making photographs that avoided the usual visual tropes and clichés. The color red, for example. He wanted to create an honest project, he says, “not this kind of post-colonial, white guy goes to China.”

2012-05-09.title: German Patience With Greece on the Euro Wears Thin
2012-05-09.abstract: Germany’s devotion to the euro runs extremely deep, but there is a growing conviction that it is up to Greece to follow through on its commitments and that Europe is done negotiating.

2012-05-09.title: Ukraine: Boycott Derails Meeting
2012-05-09.abstract: The Foreign Ministry announced that it would postpone a summit meeting of Central and Eastern European countries because many leaders planned to boycott it to protest the treatment of an opposition leader.

2012-05-10.title: Putin to Skip Group of 8 Session, Delaying Postelection Meeting With Obama
2012-05-10.abstract: Russia’s newly inaugurated president, Vladimir V. Putin, will not attend a summit meeting of world leaders next week, the White House said on Wednesday.

2012-05-10.title: In Ciudad Juárez, a New Emotion
2012-05-10.abstract: Signs that Ciudad Juárez is on the road to recovery after years of an onslaught by drug-trafficking cartels include the young people who have joined art collectives in lieu of gangs and the recently reopened nightclubs that are drawing crowds out after dark.

2012-05-10.title: Slivers of Hope Amid the Melancholy in a Mexican Border Town
2012-05-10.abstract: Ciudad Juárez is still a violent city, but homicide rates have decreased significantly from their peak in 2010, and young people in particular are stepping out.

2012-05-10.title: Long-Running Antiterrorism Work With Saudis Led to Airline Plot’s Failure
2012-05-10.abstract: Saudi Arabia and the United States have worked closely together against a militant network in Yemen, most recently foiling an effort to smuggle a bomb onto a jetliner.

2012-05-10.title: A Death Rattles a Palestinian City
2012-05-10.abstract: The death of the governor of the Jenin region has alarmed Palestinians worried about the return of violence to a city widely regarded as a model for the West Bank.

2012-05-11.title: China’s Growth Slows, and Its Political Model Shows Limits
2012-05-11.abstract: Signs of a slowing economy and recent political upheavals in China add urgency to questions of how much control the state should exert, in business and in the lives of citizens.

2012-05-11.title: Egyptian Candidates Clash in TV Debate, an Arab First
2012-05-11.abstract: Two of the leading candidates to become Egypt’s next president traded barbs about their political histories and arguments about the role of religion in public life.

2012-05-11.title: Pentagon Stresses Efforts to Recover U.S. Soldier Being Held in Afghanistan
2012-05-11.abstract: Pentagon officials emphasized that the government is working to locate Sgt. Bowe Bergdahl, who is being held captive by Afghan insurgents.

2012-05-11.title: Ex-Official’s Drive in China Leads to Torture Inquiry
2012-05-11.abstract: The review of police actions appears to be part of a deeper critical look at Bo Xilai’s four-year reign in Chongqing.

2012-05-11.title: European Countries Seek Easing of Provision Included in Iranian Oil Embargo
2012-05-11.abstract: Britain and other European Union members fear that a ban on insurers covering Iranian oil ships could have harmful economic effects.

2012-05-12.title: Nuclear Negotiator Seeks ‘Beginnings of the End’ of Iran Dispute
2012-05-12.abstract: Catherine Ashton, the lead negotiator for the six-nation group bargaining with Iran over its contentious uranium enrichment program, did not offer specifics about talks on May 23 in Baghdad.

2012-05-12.title: Crossword Puzzle Stirs Controversy in Venezuela
2012-05-12.abstract: Neptalí Segovia, a veteran crossword writer, is accused of hiding a coded message urging the death of President Hugo Chavez’s brother, who is the governor of Barinas State.

2012-05-12.title: A Russian Protest Leader Takes Center Stage
2012-05-12.abstract: Russia’s protest movement appears to have entered a new and unpredictable stage, and Sergei Udaltsov, one of its most prominent leaders, is at the center of a debate about its future.

2012-05-12.title: Egyptian Campaign Focuses on Islam’s Role in Public Life
2012-05-12.abstract: The main candidates have said that security and the economy are the most important issues to voters, but a debate returned repeatedly to questions about religious influence.

2012-05-12.title: Leftist Party’s Rise Upends Greek Political Order
2012-05-12.abstract: Alexis Tsipras, the leader of the party Syriza, hopes to ride a tide of anti-austerity sentiment to break from four decades of ossified two-party government in Greece.

2012-05-13.title: Gunmen in Afghan Police Uniforms Kill 2 NATO Soldiers
2012-05-13.abstract: NATO did not confirm the nationality of the victims, but a spokesman for the governor of Helmand Province said that they were British soldiers.

2012-05-13.title: Echoing Out of Texas, Chinese Voice of Dissent for Religious Freedom
2012-05-13.abstract: Bob Fu, a Chinese-born pastor who started the dissident group ChinaAid, was instrumental in calling attention to the plight of the activist Chen Guangcheng.

2012-05-13.title: Amid Signs of Economic Weaknesses, China to Free Cash for Lending
2012-05-13.abstract: After this week’s disappointing economic figures, China’s central bank said Saturday that it would reduce the share of deposits banks must set aside as reserves.

2012-05-13.title: U.S. May Scrap Costly Efforts to Train Iraqi Police
2012-05-13.abstract: The State Department could jettison a multibillion-dollar training effort by the end of 2012 that has emerged as the latest high-profile example of America’s waning influence in the country.

2012-05-13.title: A Tiger in Love
2012-05-13.abstract: A review of “I Am an Executioner,” a first collection of unpredictable short stories by Rajesh Parameswaran.

2012-05-14.title: Police Find 49 Bodies by a Highway in Mexico
2012-05-14.abstract: A message near the scene suggested the Zetas drug cartel was responsible for the deaths of 6 women and 43 men.

2012-05-14.title: Williams Team Gets First Win Since ’04, Then Fire Strikes
2012-05-14.abstract: Pastor Maldonado took his first Formula One victory. Then, a little over an hour after the race, as the team celebrated, there was an explosion followed by a fire in the team’s garage.

2012-05-14.title: Wang Lijun, Chen Guangcheng and Western Amnesia
2012-05-14.abstract: In his latest Page Two column, Anand Giridharadas argues that the West is not remembering its own history when it scolds China for abuses. And he says that, in its way, China is opening up, bit by bit.

2012-05-14.title: Thousands of Libyans Struggle With Recovery of Property Confiscated by Qaddafi
2012-05-14.abstract: The vast policy of land distribution begun by Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi in the 1970s is threatening to ignite a patchwork of bitter property disputes.

2012-05-14.title: Period of Relative Calm in Iraq Is Brought to End by Fatal Bomb Blasts
2012-05-14.abstract: A few years ago, the day’s toll would have been a footnote in Iraq, when it was not uncommon for 100 people to be killed in one day.

2012-05-15.title: Beyond the Sandbags and Steel, 12 Lanes to Another Afghanistan
2012-05-15.abstract: The story of Strikers, the country’s only bowling alley, seems to be that rarest of things, an Afghan good news story.

2012-05-15.title: Syrians Defy Leaders to Aid Those in Need
2012-05-15.abstract: An increasingly organized underground network is providing food and medicine to people trapped by the offensive of President Bashar al-Assad’s government.

2012-05-15.title: 23 Syrian Soldiers Reported Killed by Rebels
2012-05-15.abstract: While opposition forces said they had repulsed an attack on the city of Rastan, spillover sectarian fighting continued in northern Lebanon.

2012-05-15.title: Saudi Arabia Seeks Union of Monarchies in Region
2012-05-15.abstract: The conservative monarchy wants to form a single federation with its five Persian Gulf neighbors to better resist the waves of change sweeping the Middle East.

2012-05-15.title: Risk of Greek Euro Exit Rattles Markets, but Hints of More Talks Emerge
2012-05-15.abstract: The latest turmoil could signify a new round of financial instability for Europe and beyond, but could also spark a new phase of bargaining between Greece and its European lenders.

2012-05-16.title: A Murder Trial in Sweden, With Echoes of Norway
2012-05-16.abstract: Peter Mangs is on trial in the killings of three people and the attempted murder of 12 others in a case that reminds Swedes of the Norwegian massacre last summer.

2012-05-16.title: A Dam Clouds The Future of Peru’s Indigenous People
2012-05-16.abstract: On the eastern slopes of the Andes in Peru, home mainly to indigenous peoples like the Ashaninka, the government wants to dam the Ene River and sell most of the hydroelectric power to Brazil.

2012-05-16.title: Dam Project Threatens a Way of Life in Peru
2012-05-16.abstract: A hydroelectric dam supported by the country’s president would send thousands of people to live elsewhere.

2012-05-16.title: Numb to Carnage, Mexicans Find Diversions, and Life Goes On
2012-05-16.abstract: Many Mexicans are increasingly disturbed by their disaffection as drug violence has taken a turn for the worse.

2012-05-16.title: Afghan Soldiers Increasingly Attack American Counterparts
2012-05-16.abstract: The rise in “green on blue” killings — Afghan forces turning on their American trainers — has threatened the goal of leaving Afghanistan in the hands of a stable security force.

2012-05-17.title: D.E.A.’s Agents Join Counternarcotics Efforts in Honduras
2012-05-17.abstract: The Central American country is a growing focus of American efforts aimed at drug cartels that have sought to use its ungoverned spaces.

2012-05-17.title: Canadian Police Violated Laws in G-20 Sweep, Inquiry Finds
2012-05-17.abstract: Police officers jailed people illegally and escalated violence at protests surrounding the Group of 20 meeting in Toronto two years ago, an independent review concluded Wednesday.

2012-05-17.title: After 7 Years, No End in Sight to Phone Hacking Scandal
2012-05-17.abstract: The scandal that has shaken Rupert Murdoch’s global media empire is now the focus of three Scotland Yard inquires and more than 100 civil lawsuits.

2012-05-17.title: Egypt’s Military Leader Promises a Fair Election
2012-05-17.abstract: Field Marshal Mohamed Hussein Tantawi spoke amid concerns about the potential for voting fraud and the military’s willingness to shift to civilian control.

2012-05-17.title: In China, Female Boxers Strike a Blow
2012-05-17.abstract: For 305 women gathered at the Women’s World Boxing Championships in Qinhuangdao, a seaside town about three hours’ drive east of Beijing, shorts ruled. And fists flew. The championships are historic, with women’s boxing debuting as an Olympic event at the London Games.

2012-05-18.title: A Bridge-Building, Cross-Cultural Art Project That’s Also Delicious
2012-05-18.abstract: An international collaboration between chefs, part of the Havana Biennial, combines a boundary-crossing art project with the simple delight of complicated food.

2012-05-18.title: ‘Princelings’ in China Use Family Ties to Gain Riches
2012-05-18.abstract: The authorities are eager to paint the fallen official Bo Xilai, whose family has a substantial fortune, as a rogue operator. But other officials’ relatives have also amassed vast wealth.

2012-05-18.title: Power and Profit in China’s Economic Boom
2012-05-18.abstract: Family members of many high-ranking officials have profited enormously from China’s economic boom.

2012-05-18.title: Thousands of Myanmar Rohingyas Struggle for Refugee Status in India
2012-05-18.abstract: The stateless ethnic minority group has suffered generations of persecution, which has roots in British colonialism.

2012-05-18.title: Italy Steps Up Security Forces
2012-05-18.abstract: The move came after months of growing social tensions stemming from Italy’s economic crisis.

2012-05-19.title: Heading Into Talks With Iran, U.S. Sees Hopeful Signs
2012-05-19.abstract: As American negotiators prepare for nuclear talks next week in Baghdad, they say recent statements from Iran and pressure from sanctions have raised the chances of compromise.

2012-05-19.title: From a Honduras Hospital, Conflicting Tales of a Riverside Shootout
2012-05-19.abstract: Some Honduran officials and residents have said that the victims of a fatal gun battle last week were innocent people, challenging the account of American drug enforcement agents.

2012-05-19.title: Rising Greek Political Star, Foe of Austerity, Puts Europe on Edge
2012-05-19.abstract: The left-wing leader Alexis Tsipras, who pledges to repudiate Greece’s austerity-for-loans bailout deal, says the country’s problem is a European one, necessitating a European solution.

2012-05-19.title: Luxury Liner’s Removal to Begin Off Italian Coast
2012-05-19.abstract: The Costa Concordia, the cruise ship that ran aground in January, will be lifted onto a platform beginning next week and then towed away to be demolished.

2012-05-19.title: Crackdown on Students Stokes Fury Within Syria
2012-05-19.abstract: Antigovernment anger in Syria has escalated over the harsh repression of students in Aleppo, where activists on Friday reported extensive protests against President Bashar al-Assad.

2012-05-20.title: A Deafening Silence About Chen in China
2012-05-20.abstract: With a few notable — if hard to find — exceptions, microblogs, Web sites and traditional media ignored the arrival of Chen Guangcheng in New York, or were censored. But the campaign against ‘foreign trash’ continued.

2012-05-20.title: NATO Summit Opens With No Deal on Pakistan Supply Lines
2012-05-20.abstract: A NATO summit meeting on long-term security for Afghanistan opened in Chicago with the United States and Pakistan still divided over reopening supply routes for the war.

2012-05-20.title: Thousands Are Homeless in Deadly Quake in Italy
2012-05-20.abstract: An earthquake struck the northern Italian region of Emilia Romagna on Sunday, killing five people, injuring dozens and damaging historic buildings as well as warehouses and factories.

2012-05-20.title: Megrahi, Convicted in 1988 Lockerbie Bombing, Dies at 60
2012-05-20.abstract: Abdel Basset Ali al-Megrahi was the only person convicted in the attack on Pan Am Flight 103 over Lockerbie, Scotland, which killed 270 people.

2012-05-20.title: Nationalist Wins Serbian Presidency, Clouding Ties to the West
2012-05-20.abstract: Tomislav Nikolic, an ardent admirer of Russia, won 49.8 percent of the vote compared with 47 percent for Boris Tadic, the former president.

2012-05-21.title: Former Colombian Hostage Is Suspected of Helping Plan His Kidnapping
2012-05-21.abstract: Sigifredo López, who was kidnapped along with 11 other legislators in 2002, has been detained under suspicion of helping the FARC plan their abduction.

2012-05-21.title: Libyan’s Death Brings Up Debate Over His Release
2012-05-21.abstract: Controversy arose again over who was ultimately responsible for the Lockerbie bombing and whether Abdel Basset Ali al-Megrahi was among them, or a scapegoat.

2012-05-21.title: International Agency Will Try to Resume Talks With Iran on Nuclear Program
2012-05-21.abstract: Officials from the International Atomic Energy Agency aim to resume talks on the proposed inspection of a building they suspect Iran used in testing explosives that can trigger a nuclear blast.

2012-05-21.title: Egypt’s Choice
2012-05-21.abstract: Egypt’s once-opaque politics are a rich public spectacle, bubbling with talk of candidates, their missteps and their plans.

2012-05-21.title: In Streets and Online, Campaign Fever in Egypt
2012-05-21.abstract: Three days before Egypt’s presidential elections, a combination of high stakes, suspense and confusion has made for a heady atmosphere.

2012-05-22.title: France: Strauss-Kahn Faces a New Investigation
2012-05-22.abstract: The prosecutor’s office in Lille said Monday that it had opened a preliminary investigation into allegations that Dominique Strauss-Kahn sexually assaulted a woman at a Washington hotel in 2010.

2012-05-22.title: China: North Koreans Free Detained Fishermen
2012-05-22.abstract: At least 28 Chinese fishermen who had been detained by North Korean gunmen for 13 days returned home on Monday, according to reports in the Chinese state news media.

2012-05-22.title: Late for School After a Long Journey for a Drop to Drink
2012-05-22.abstract: In drought-racked Niger, the difficult search for water frequently falls to children as young as 10, and they often arrive late to school, or not at all.

2012-05-22.title: France to Press Germany on Euro Bonds and Other Economic Measures
2012-05-22.abstract: At a meeting on Wednesday with European leaders, President François Hollande of France will press the issue of euro bonds, which some officials consider a step too far.

2012-05-22.title: Secret Clinics Tend to Bahrain’s Wounded
2012-05-22.abstract: Amid an uprising, “no one goes to the hospital,” one protester said. The police are there.

2012-05-23.title: Hamas-Run Schools Set Out to Teach ‘the Language of the Enemy’
2012-05-23.abstract: Students in Gaza will soon have the option of taking Hebrew classes for the first time in nearly two decades.

2012-05-23.title: Iran Recalls Its Ambassador From Azerbaijan
2012-05-23.abstract: The ambassador in Baku was summoned home amid a series of protests and rising tensions over matters as varied as weapons purchases and the Eurovision song contest.

2012-05-23.title: Russia Demands Inquiry in Death of Child in U.S.
2012-05-23.abstract: The United States government tried to head off a diplomatic row with Russia regarding an investigation into the death of a Russian child in an adoptive American family.

2012-05-23.title: Argentina: Bomb Was Set to Strike Former President of Colombia
2012-05-23.abstract: A bomb discovered in a Buenos Aires theater on Tuesday was programmed to explode when the former president of Colombia, Álvaro Uribe, was scheduled to speak there on Wednesday, authorities said.

2012-05-23.title: China: Tycoon Gets Reprieve
2012-05-23.abstract: A self-made tycoon sentenced to death for financial fraud has been resentenced to death with a two-year reprieve, meaning her sentence will probably be changed to life after two years of good behavior.

2012-05-24.title: China: General’s Trip to Japan Is Canceled
2012-05-24.abstract: One of the top generals in China, Guo Boxiong, has canceled a high-level trip to Japan scheduled for this week after a rise in political tensions between the two nations.

2012-05-24.title: Honduran Villages Caught in Drug War’s Cross-Fire
2012-05-24.abstract: Despite an influx of American assistance, residents of an area of Honduras that has become a way station for cocaine on its way north say they feel threatened.

2012-05-24.title: Turkmenistan: Gas Deal Signed With India and Pakistan
2012-05-24.abstract: Three countries involved in a plan to build a natural gas pipeline across Afghanistan signed an agreement on Wednesday in Avaza, Turkmenistan.

2012-05-24.title: Eurovision Takes Baku
2012-05-24.abstract: The glamour of Eurovision overshadows the human rights issues that mar Azerbaijan.

2012-05-24.title: Welcoming Eurovision, but Not the Scrutiny
2012-05-24.abstract: Azerbaijan’s government has gone all out to prepare for the Eurovision song contest, and rights groups have seized the chance to spotlight the country’s record of abuses.

2012-05-25.title: Building a Dam in Lebanon
2012-05-25.abstract: Lebanese Christians want a dam to exploit their natural resources and to help alleviate their chronic shortages of electricity and water, but the only donor stepping forward is Iran.

2012-05-25.title: Iran Is Seeking Lebanon Stake as Syria Totters
2012-05-25.abstract: Iran’s eagerness to shower money on Lebanon is the latest indication of just how worried Tehran is at the prospect that Syria’s leader could fall.

2012-05-25.title: China Lawyer, in U.S., Voices Fears for His Family
2012-05-25.abstract: Chen Guangcheng also expressed concern about the friends and dissidents who aided his escape in April from a home that the police had turned into a virtual prison.

2012-05-25.title: Leaders of Moscow Protest Are Released, but Criminal Charges Remain Possible
2012-05-25.abstract: Aleksei Navalny and Sergei Udaltsov were questioned as a witness in a criminal investigation opened on charges of inciting mass disorder and using force against the police.

2012-05-25.title: Libya Agrees to Inquiry by Britain
2012-05-25.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron said he had won an agreement for a Scotland Yard team to conduct an investigation in Libya into the killing by Libyan diplomats of a London police officer in 1984.

2012-05-26.title: Germany Looks to Its Own Costly Reunification in Resisting Stimulus for Greece
2012-05-26.abstract: The German reluctance to underwrite struggling economies like Greece’s is based on its own experience of pouring $2 trillion into the former East Germany, to little immediate effect.

2012-05-26.title: Brazil: President Vetoes Major Parts of Bill to Open Up Forests
2012-05-26.abstract: President Dilma Rousseff on Friday vetoed portions of Brazil’s new Forest Code, a bill drafted to open big areas of protected forests to large-scale agriculture.

2012-05-26.title: Russia: Free Apartments for Injured Police Officers
2012-05-26.abstract: The Moscow city government will provide apartments to four police officers injured during a brawl with protesters, in another demonstration of officials’ frustration with the antigovernment movement.

2012-05-26.title: Israel Is Anxiously Watching Egyptian Vote
2012-05-26.abstract: Israeli analysts are bracing for the prospect of a less-friendly Egypt, especially if Mohamed Morsi, the candidate of the Muslim Brotherhood, is elected president.

2012-05-26.title: Shades of Guilt in One Soldier’s Purple Heart
2012-05-26.abstract: For one decorated soldier, the metal on his lapel that represents the capacity to survive, endure and sacrifice also carries with it a darker side, including the shadows of the lives taken.

2012-05-27.title: U.S. Efforts Fail to Curtail Trade in Afghan Opium
2012-05-27.abstract: Some officials privately concede that there is little chance for large-scale success of the anti-opium effort in Afghanistan before the end of the NATO military mission in 2014.

2012-05-27.title: Tunisia Salafis Riot to Protest an Arrest
2012-05-27.abstract: Hundreds of hard-line Islamists rampaged through the town of Jendouba, attacking a police station and stores selling alcohol, said the official news agency.

2012-05-27.title: African Style Goes Global, Despite Little Tangible Support From African Leaders
2012-05-27.abstract: The presidents of Nigeria and Ghana talk a good game to Italian Men’s Vogue this month, but it’s African designers who are forcing their work onto global runways.

2012-05-27.title: State of Paradox
2012-05-27.abstract: Rapid change, Akash Kapur explains, has brought contrast and contradiction to India.

2012-05-27.title: Inquiry Ordered Into Deaths of Afghan Family
2012-05-27.abstract: So far this month, 33 NATO service members have been killed in Afghanistan, about half as many as died in May 2011.

2012-05-28.title: Azerbaijan Delights in Taking the Stage as Eurovision Host
2012-05-28.abstract: Eager to make its debut on the world stage, the Azerbaijan government went all out to ensure a smooth, secure event.

2012-05-28.title: West Point Is Divided on a War Doctrine’s Fate
2012-05-28.abstract: Faculty at the United States Military Academy are debating what a counterinsurgency strategy achieved in Iraq and Afghanistan and whether the doctrine has a future.

2012-05-28.title: Legislature in Nepal Disbands in Failure
2012-05-28.abstract: Nepal descended into a new crisis on Sunday after rival political parties failed to reach an agreement on a new constitution before the national legislature’s term expired at midnight.

2012-05-28.title: Londoners Dread Traffic as City Plans Olympics
2012-05-28.abstract: Officials have warned commuters and set up infrastructure to handle its visitors, but some worry the Games may prove too much for the city’s roads and subway.

2012-05-28.title: Egyptian Is Counting on Worries of Elites
2012-05-28.abstract: The expected runoff between Ahmed Shafik, a Mubarak associate, and Mohamed Morsi, of the Muslim Brotherhood, is a rematch of an old struggle between secular authoritarians and Islamists.

2012-05-29.title: Greek Voters Tilt to Right, but Many Urge Caution
2012-05-29.abstract: A right-leaning political party that has largely advocated staying the course under the austerity plan has surged ahead of its leftist competitor in popularity.

2012-05-29.title: Where the United States Lags Far Behind Canada
2012-05-29.abstract: The United States prides itself on its tolerance and its exceptionalism, but when it comes to equal marriage rights for gays and lesbians, not so much.

2012-05-29.title: Virtual Therapy Helps Residents of a Shellshocked City
2012-05-29.abstract: Twenty-five patients in violence-racked Ciudad Juárez completed a therapy program similar to the one used by the United States military to treat Iraq war veterans with post-traumatic stress disorder.

2012-05-29.title: Former Beijing Mayor Denies Responsibility for Tiananmen Square Crackdown
2012-05-29.abstract: In a new book, Chen Xitong denies years of accusations that he turned Deng Xiaoping against students and then played a key role in organizing the military assault.

2012-05-29.title: Russian Foreign Ministry Criticizes American Envoy
2012-05-29.abstract: In an unusual statement, the ministry said a talk American Ambassador Michael A. McFaul gave to Russian students last week “went far beyond the boundaries of diplomatic etiquette.”

2012-05-30.title: After Scandal in Soccer, Italy’s Leader Urges Break
2012-05-30.abstract: Prime Minister Mario Monti suggested that soccer be suspended for two or three years after a match-fixing scandal shook the country’s most beloved sport this week.

2012-05-30.title: Romney Calls for Action on Syria, but His Party Is Divided
2012-05-30.abstract: Mitt Romney criticized President Obama’s opposition to arming Syrian rebels. Some Republicans share that reluctance, while others want to go further, urging airstrikes.

2012-05-30.title: Supporters and Critics of Annan See Crisis in Syria as a Threat to His Legacy
2012-05-30.abstract: After excelling in diplomacy for years, Kofi Annan is being criticized for his peace plan for Syria, which continues to have violence despite a cease-fire.

2012-05-30.title: Adding to Unease of a Drug War Alliance
2012-05-30.abstract: The arrests of four formerly high-ranking officers have reawakened American concerns about how closely the Mexican Army may be linked to a leading cocaine cartel.

2012-05-30.title: India’s Economy Slows, With Global Implications
2012-05-30.abstract: India’s problems have dampened hopes that it, along with China and other non-Western economies, might help revive global growth.

2012-05-31.title: For the White House, a Wary Wait as Syria Boils
2012-05-31.abstract: President Obama is loath to engage in new military actions, but with each passing incident, the scale of the crisis in Syria grows.

2012-05-31.title: Amid Uproar, Graphic Painting of South African President Is Removed From Gallery
2012-05-31.abstract: Controversy erupted after a painting depicting South Africa’s president, Jacob Zuma, with his genitals exposed was exhibited at a Johannesburg gallery.

2012-05-31.title: Brazil’s Political Class Jolted by Claim That Ex-Leader Pressed a High Court Judge
2012-05-31.abstract: Judge Gilmar Mendes asserted that the former president, Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, put pressure on him to delay a trial over a vote-buying scandal involving the governing Workers Party.

2012-05-31.title: Colombia: Rebels Release Journalist
2012-05-31.abstract: Colombia’s largest rebel group on Wednesday released a French freelance journalist whom it had held captive for more than a month.

2012-05-31.title: Tibetan Woman Dies in Self-Immolation
2012-05-31.abstract: A Tibetan mother of three set fire to herself on Wednesday outside a monastery in the town of Barma, an advocacy group reported.
